SourceProxy.label: SHYX Adaptive Isotropic Remesher
SourceProxy.name: SHYXAdaptiveIsotropicRemesher

Documentation.short_help: Curvature-aware CGAL isotropic remesh (Vespa ICC sizing).

SourceProxy.Documentation:
Adaptive isotropic remeshing using **Vespa FeatureAwareAdaptiveSizingField** (CGAL `isotropic_remeshing` plus custom interpolated corrected curvatures — not CGAL stock Adaptive_sizing_field). Edge lengths are bounded by Min/Max Edge Length; Adaptive Tolerance controls fineness together with curvature. The remesh face subset is chosen either from an optional vtkSelection on port 1 or from a scalar value range on the input (see Remesh region); when that subset is empty, the whole surface is remeshed. Edges on the boundary between remeshed and untouched triangles are constrained as feature edges (independent of the Detect feature edges master toggle).
Detect feature edges (master toggle): when ON (default), CGAL detect_sharp_edges and feature-mask region/boundary contributions are added to the constraint maps for remesh. When OFF, those sources are skipped; only selection / scalar-range patch boundaries constrain remeshing, and output port 1 is empty.
Outputs: 0 Remeshed surface (no **VespaIccNonMaskVertexNormal** on this port; use port 3 preview for ICC normals). 1 Sharp/feature edges as **lines** only (no vertex cells). Empty when Detect feature edges is OFF. 2 Input geometry, faces passing the feature mask threshold. 3 Sizing / ICC preview on the mesh **before the last remesh iteration** (same as input topology when NumberOfIterations is 1). By default Recompute curvature each iteration is ON — maps match a refresh immediately before that last iteration. Turn it OFF only to trade accuracy for speed. `v:vespa_icc_normal` stays internal. Feature-mask dual ICC normals on port 3: iterations==1 use the input mask; iterations>1 probe mask fields onto the preview (same mask-driven ICC prep as the remesh pass; requires **Transfer input arrays to output** / UpdateAttributes ON). Capped VespaAdaptiveSizeGlobal from `v:vespa_size_global`; principal curvatures and uncapped size from a second ICC pass on the preview geometry. **VespaIccNonMaskVertexNormal** (port 3 only) matches non-mask ICC vertex normals (`v:vespa_icc_n_nonmask`), or global blend when absent. Default coloring on port 3: **VespaAdaptiveSizeGlobal**.
Requires CGAL 6.0+.

InputProperty.name: Selection
InputProperty.label: Selection
InputProperty.Documentation: Optional vtkSelection used when Remesh region source is "Selection (port 1)" (default). When present and non-empty, CGAL remeshing is applied only to the indicated faces (same convention as SHYX Delete Selected Cells). If the port is unset or the selection yields no cells, the whole surface is remeshed. When Remesh region source is "Scalar range on input", this port is ignored for choosing the remesh subset. The boundary between remeshed and untouched faces is constrained as feature edges for remesh (with Protection Angle / Feature Mask rules).

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshRegionMode
IntVectorProperty.label: Remesh region source
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: How the patch of faces passed to CGAL isotropic remeshing is chosen. Selection uses the vtkSelection input when it resolves to cells. Scalar range includes all cells whose chosen array tuple magnitude lies in [Remesh range min, Remesh range max] (inclusive), with the same point- vs cell-array rules as Feature Mask; port 1 is then ignored for this subset.

StringVectorProperty.name: RemeshRangeArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Range array
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Point- or cell-centered array on the input. If both exist with the same name, cell data is used. A cell is in the remesh region when tuple magnitude is between Remesh range min and max (inclusive). Stored on VTK input-array slot 0 for ParaView ArrayRangeDomain.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: RemeshRangeMin
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Remesh range min
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Inclusive lower bound on tuple magnitude for cells in the remesh region (scalar range mode). Domain min/max follow the selected array; use **Reset** next to the property to adopt the suggested bound.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: RemeshRangeMax
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Remesh range max
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Inclusive upper bound; must be >= Remesh range min. Domain min/max follow the selected array; use **Reset** to adopt the suggested bound.

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshRangeAllScalars
IntVectorProperty.label: Range: all corners (points)
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When the range array is point-centered: if ON, every corner of a cell must fall inside the range; if OFF, one in-range corner is enough. Ignored for cell-centered arrays.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: MinEdgeLength
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Min edge length
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Minimum edge length; must be > 0. ParaView BoundsDomain (scaled_extent 0.001) suggests 0.1% of the input axis-aligned bounding box longest side; use Scale/Reset next to the property.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: MaxEdgeLength
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Max edge length

DoubleVectorProperty.name: AdaptiveTolerance
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Adaptive tolerance
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Vespa ICC adaptive sizing tolerance (global / non-feature dispatch to `v:vespa_size_global`). Smaller values tend to refine more within the Min/Max edge length bounds.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: AdaptiveSizingNeighborMaxRatio
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Neighbor max size ratio
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Caps spatial jumps of the Vespa ICC sizing maps: **≤ 1** disables (raw per-vertex ICC targets). Default **1.6** relaxes `v:vespa_size_global` and `v:vespa_size_feature` so along any mesh edge the larger vertex target does not exceed R times the smaller (e.g. 1.3–2 to tame abrupt coarsening next to fine regions). Set **0** to disable.

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshRecomputeCurvatureEachIteration
IntVectorProperty.label: Recompute curvature each iteration
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When ON (default), each remesh iteration runs separately with FeatureAwareAdaptiveSizingField::recompute_curvature on the updated mesh (**full surface** ICC after the initial pass). When OFF, ICC is computed once in the Vespa sizing constructor before remesh iterations; splits inherit targets by averaging incident neighbors (`register_split_vertex`). Turning OFF can be faster when Remesh iterations is large.

IntVectorProperty.name: NumberOfRelaxationSteps
IntVectorProperty.label: Relaxation steps
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL vertex-relocation steps per remesh iteration (higher = smoother surface; 0 disables relaxation).

IntVectorProperty.name: DetectFeatureEdges
IntVectorProperty.label: Detect feature edges
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Master switch for sharp-edge / feature-mask constraints. When ON (default), CGAL detect_sharp_edges (with Protection angle / Sharp side filter) and feature-mask region / boundary contributions are added to edge_is_constrained_map for isotropic_remeshing. When OFF, none of those sources contribute; remesh only sees vtkSelection-boundary constraints (when a selection input is connected). vtkSelection behavior is independent of this toggle. Output port 1 is empty when OFF.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: ProtectAngle
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Protection angle
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Sharp-edge angle threshold in degrees (CGAL detect_sharp_edges). Endpoints of detected sharp edges are protected during remesh.

IntVectorProperty.name: SharpFeatureSideFilter
IntVectorProperty.label: Sharp side filter
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Optional filter on interior sharp edges after CGAL detect_sharp_edges, using signed dihedral angle (approximate_dihedral_angle). Excludes concave (signed < 0) or convex (signed > 0) from remesh constraints and from port 1. Boundary edges are unchanged. If convex/concave looks swapped, flip mesh orientation or pick the other entry.

IntVectorProperty.name: FeatureMaskEnabled
IntVectorProperty.label: Use feature mask
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When ON: threshold input faces to port 2; port 1 sharp features are computed only on that patch; on the full surface, sharp edges are intersected with the mask for CGAL remesh. Choose a point- or cell-centered array, a threshold (tuple magnitude > threshold), and the optional All Scalars flag for point arrays. Has no effect when Detect feature edges is OFF.

StringVectorProperty.name: FeatureMaskArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Array
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Name of a point- or cell-centered array. If both exist with the same name, cell data is used. Tuple magnitude must be > Threshold for a cell (or per-point corner) to pass.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: FeatureMaskThreshold
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Threshold
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Cells pass when tuple magnitude is strictly greater than this value (scalar or vector magnitude). Typical: 0 to keep values > 0.

IntVectorProperty.name: FeatureMaskAllScalars
IntVectorProperty.label: All scalars
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When the chosen array is point-centered: if ON, every corner of a cell must pass the magnitude test; if OFF, one passing corner is enough (vtkThreshold-style). Ignored when the array resolves to cell data.

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshProtectConstraints
IntVectorProperty.label: Protect constraints
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL `protect_constraints`. When ON, feature edges are neither split nor collapsed. When OFF, constrained edges may be split or collapsed per CGAL (subject to Collapse constraints below).

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshCollapseConstraints
IntVectorProperty.label: Collapse constraints
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL `collapse_constraints`. When Protect constraints is OFF, allows collapsing constrained edges. Ignored by CGAL when Protect constraints is ON.

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshRelaxConstraints
IntVectorProperty.label: Relax constraints
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL `relax_constraints`. When ON, endpoints of constrained and boundary edges may move along constrained polylines during tangential relaxation.

IntVectorProperty.name: UseUpdateAttributes
IntVectorProperty.label: Interpolate attributes
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: If ON, point/cell data are interpolated onto port 0 (and probed onto port 3 ICC preview when Remesh iterations > 1 with Feature mask enabled, so mask-driven ICC on the preview matches the evolving geometry).
